finding: `npm run build` failed with `ATM_RUNNER_SYNC_QUEUE_HEAD_REQUIRED`, but `details.requiredCommand` was a prose reason instead of the runnable broker enqueue command. The operator had to inspect `broker --help` and source references to discover `broker runner-sync enqueue`.
expectedBehavior: Runner-sync admission failures should emit a copyable `requiredCommand` that includes `node atm.mjs broker runner-sync enqueue --task <task-id> --actor <actor-id> --sealed-source-sha <sha> --surface release/atm-onefile/atm.mjs --surface release/atm-root-drop --json`, plus the matching release command once a steward id exists.
evidenceOrRepro: During ATM-GOV-0185 dogfood, `ATM_RETAIN_RELEASE_ARTIFACTS=1 npm run build` returned `requiredCommand: "runner sync requires a broker runner-sync queue-head reservation before build or internal-release sync"`; the working command was `node atm.mjs broker runner-sync enqueue --task ATM-GOV-0185 --actor codex-governance-optimizer --sealed-source-sha 46b16442082c88f4554e479feae726b22d6b4271 --surface release/atm-onefile/atm.mjs --surface release/atm-root-drop --json`.
followUp: Update runner-sync admission diagnostics and tests so build-time failures always expose runnable enqueue/release commands and actor identity hints.
